input.instructions:
Solve this Super-Eywa grading-bank question.
Question ID: coding-B1-usaco-2024-us-open-platinum-identity-theft
Title: B1. USACO 2024 US Open Platinum / Identity Theft

Problem:
Extend cows' bitstring IDs so no cow can be mistaken for another when some reported IDs are truncated.

Grading target:
Binary hidden-test acceptance.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>

variables.selected_prompt_text:
Rewrite the problem so the next agent first checks whether the smallest candidate answer values are even possible, using modular arithmetic or direct contradiction before searching more broadly.

Return exactly one JSON object in this format:
{
  "schema_name": "eywa_node_response",
  "schema_version": "v1",
  "orchestration_decision": "transmute",
  "decision_notes": "optional string",
  "message_for_next_agent": "plain-text reformulation for the next agent"
}
variables.child_selected_prompt_text:
Solve the question directly.
Keep the entire JSON compact.
Keep decision_notes extremely short. Prefer 3 to 8 words.
Do not narrate your reasoning outside the response field.
In the response field, use exactly two short lines encoded with \n:
FINAL_ANSWER: <answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>
Keep the justification to 12 words or fewer.
Use plain text only inside the response field. Do not use markdown formatting, code fences, or math-style backslashes such as \equiv.
Do not add any extra text before or after the JSON object.

Return exactly one JSON object in this format:
{
  "schema_name": "eywa_node_response",
  "schema_version": "v1",
  "orchestration_decision": "execute_locally",
  "decision_notes": "optional string",
  "response": "FINAL_ANSWER: <answer>\nJUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>",
  "result_type": "summary"
}

orchestration.decision_notes: Reformulated the prompt to enforce a 'feasibility check' phase using modular arithmetic or contradiction to prune the search space before the agent attempts a full construction or search.

results.content:
Reformulated the prompt to enforce a 'feasibility check' phase using modular arithmetic or contradiction to prune the search space before the agent attempts a full construction or search.
node_root_helper_01 (transmuted_child): Solve the following problem: [coding-B1-usaco-2024-us-open-platinum-identity-theft]. 

IMPORTANT INSTRUCTION FOR SEARCH STRATEGY:
Before attempting to construct a full solution or performing a broad search for the optimal bitstring, you must first perform a feasibility check on the smallest candidate answer values. Specifically, use modular arithmetic, parity checks, or direct contradiction to determine if the smallest possible values satisfy the problem's constraints. If the smallest candidates are mathematically impossible, use that contradiction to narrow your search space immediately. Only proceed to a full construction once you have verified the mathematical possibility of the candidate range.
